fact: July 10 is exactly 191 days into the year, which means there are exactly 174 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun stays up for over 15 hours in many places, making it perfect for epic water balloon fights!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around July 10 in North America - some species buzz so loud they can damage your hearing if you get too close!
Why do fireflies blink like crazy around this time? July 10 falls right in the middle of firefly mating season when they're desperately trying to find love!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 10 is smack in the middle of winter - penguins are huddling together while kids in America are swimming!
Sunflowers are growing like monsters right now - they can shoot up 6 inches in a single week around July 10 as they race toward their full height!
The zodiac sign Cancer rules until July 22, so July 10 babies are Cancers - represented by a crab that walks sideways and pinches things!
Ocean water temperatures typically hit their warmest point of the year around this date in many coastal areas - perfect for spotting jellyfish floating near the surface!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying around July 10 because hot, humid summer weather creates perfect breeding conditions in puddles and ponds!
July 10 falls exactly one month after the summer solstice, which means daylight is still super long but starting its slow journey back toward shorter days!
Thunderstorms love this time of year in many regions - the hot ground and cool upper atmosphere create perfect conditions for lightning shows that can be seen from space!
Corn plants are often having growth spurts around July 10 - farmers say you can literally hear corn growing on hot, humid nights as the stalks crack and pop!
